chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT MIRPURKHAS Constitutional Petition No.S-205 of 2024 (Abdul Hafeez & others Vs. Province of Sindh & others) DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE Date of hearing and order 26.09.2024 Mr. Waheed Ali Lashari, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Mir Tarique Khan, advocate a/w respondents No.9 to 14. Mr. Ayaz Ali Rajpar, Assistant A.G a/w ASI Abdul Rasheed of PS Town and Inspector Ghulam Mustafa of PS Mehran. = ORDER Adnan-ul-Karim, Memon, J. Petitioner Abdul Hafeez Shoro has filed this petition under Article 199 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, seeking direction to the official respondents No.1 to 8 to provide protection and not to harass the petitioner at the behest of private respondents. 2. Learned Assistant A.G Sindh assisted by counsel for the private respondents’ alongwith police officials present in court and states that the official respondents as well as private respondents will act strictly in accordance with law and they will be neutral in the private affairs between the parties, if any. Police officials are present and state that they shall act strictly in accordance with law and provide the protection to the petitioner and his family members if any harassment is caused to them by the private respondents; however, if any cognizable offence is committed by either party, they will act swiftly in accordance with law. 3. In view of above, learned counsel for the petitioner seeks disposal of this petition in terms of the statement of learned A.A.G Sindh as well as counsel for the private respondents that no harassment shall be caused to either party. 4.

Police officials are present and state that they shall act strictly in accordance with law and provide the protection to the petitioner and his family members if any harassment is caused to them by the private respondents; however, if any cognizable offence is committed by either party, they will act swiftly in accordance with law. 3. In view of above, learned counsel for the petitioner seeks disposal of this petition in terms of the statement of learned A.A.G Sindh as well as counsel for the private respondents that no harassment shall be caused to either party. 4. Instant petition is disposed of. JUDGE *Ali Sher*
